data.title: Spike — Verify per-test status persistence design for T1/T2 snapshot triggers
data.description:
As a sprint implementer, I need the snapshot schema and per-test status persistence approach determined before implementing kudos-snapshot.sh so that T1/T2 trigger conditions are implementable with available data.

DONE DEFINITIONS:
- Examine record-test-status.sh per-test loop (lines 682+); assess feasibility of emitting per-test output without breaking existing test-gate-status format
- Option A (per-test file): if feasible, define format — .kudos/test-results.ndjson, one NDJSON line per test: {"test_file": "<path>", "status": "passed|failed|timeout", "diff_hash": "<sha>"}
- Option B (aggregate-only): if per-test infeasible, confirm snapshot schema as {"aggregate_status": "passed|failed|timeout", "tested_files": ["<path>", ...], "merge_base": "<sha>", "captured_at": "<ISO-8601>"} and document how T1/T2 detect aggregate transitions
- DECISION recorded as ticket comment on 71df-5bd8: chosen option (A or B), output format definition, impact on SC-E2-1/SC-E2-3/SC-E2-4
- Spike output: prototype shell script demonstrating chosen approach (kudos-snapshot-prototype.sh)
- Unit tests: verify prototype produces expected output for absent file, valid file, and concurrent access scenarios
